frames: The agent stands upright, then begins to lean forward and to its right, bending its knees slightly.
The agent continues to fall forward and to the right, lifting its left leg and bending its right leg, while its arms are bent at the elbows.
The agent's body is now nearly parallel to the ground, falling rapidly, with its arms extending outwards.
The agent makes initial contact with the ground on its right side, specifically its right arm and upper torso.
The agent is fully on the ground, lying on its right side, with its right arm bent and its legs curled.
The agent remains on the ground, shifting its weight slightly, with its legs becoming more tightly bent.
The agent is still on the ground, further curling its body, with its left leg bent and its right leg slightly extended.
The agent is completely collapsed on the ground, with all limbs bent and tucked close to its torso.
